Article Title: UAE pacer Khuzaima Tanveer credits ILT20 for preparing him for international cricket
Article Text:
Khuzaima Tanveer of the Desert Vipers during the ILT20. (Photo: ILT20)

Dubai:

A lot of the learning happens during training sessions, team meetings, and conversations around the group. I was fortunate to have the likes of Sam Curran, Naseem Shah and Lockie Ferguson in our team Khuzaima Tanveer

UAE fast bowler Khuzaima Tanveer has credited the ILT20 with accelerating his development as an international cricketer, saying the competition gave him the confidence to compete at the highest level after earning his maiden call-up for the national side earlier this year.The 26-year-old, who has represented the UAE in three ODIs and two T20Is since making his debut in April, said the franchise league played a key role in preparing him for international cricket through regular exposure to elite players."Representing the UAE was a proud moment for me and it's something every young cricketer dreams about. Getting my UAE passport and then going on to play for the national team were very special milestones for me and my family. The ILT20 helped prepare me for that level because you're constantly competing against high-quality players. It gives you confidence that you belong on the international stage," Tanveer said.Tanveer first caught the eye during the third season of the tournament with figures of 4/22 against the Sharjah Warriorz.Across two seasons with the Desert Vipers, he has claimed 24 wickets in 18 matches and finished with 17 wickets during the franchise's title-winning campaign in Season 4, making him the second-highest wicket-taking UAE player in the competition and third overall.He was also shortlisted for the Blue Belt award, presented to the tournament's best UAE player, which was won by Muhammad Waseem.The right-arm pacer said one of the biggest benefits of the tournament had been the opportunity to learn from established international cricketers."It's not only about what happens in the matches. A lot of the learning happens during training sessions, team meetings, and conversations around the group. I was fortunate to have the likes of Sam Curran, Naseem Shah and Lockie Ferguson in our team. Even small pieces of advice from such players add up over time and help you become a better cricketer," he said.Tanveer also brushed aside comparisons between his bowling action and that of Pakistan fast bowling great Waqar Younis."I don't think anyone can compare themselves to someone like Waqar Younis. He's a legend of the game and someone every fast bowler looks up to. If people see similarities, I take it as a compliment, but my focus is on becoming the best version of myself," he said.Looking ahead to the fifth season of the ILT20, Tanveer said he hopes to build on his progress and cement his place in the UAE side."Once you represent the UAE, you carry extra responsibility. People expect more from you and you expect more from yourself. My aim is to keep improving, perform consistently, and contribute whenever I get the opportunity," he said.
Article Summary: UAE fast bowler Khuzaima Tanveer has credited the ILT20 with accelerating his development as an international cricketer. The 26-year-old has represented the UAE in three ODIs and two T20Is since making his debut in April. He said the franchise league played a key role in preparing him for international cricket through regular exposure to elite players.

Article Title: Visa setback can't stop DR Congo's iconic living statue Lumumba Vea from inspiring the team
Article Text:
Michel Kuka Mboladinga ( Photo X )

“I am a standing statue,” Michel Kuka Mboladinga told an international news outlet while waiting for his visa status to the US ahead of DR Congo’s crucial last group-stage match in Atlanta.The visa was not granted because of US restrictions due to the Ebola outbreak in the Central African country. And it meant that DR Congo – making only their second FIFA World Cup appearance after competing as the erstwhile Zaire in 1974 – did not have their most famous fan, Mboladinga in the guise of “Lumumba Vea”, in the stands when they made history by beating Uzbekistan 3-1 and booking a place in the knockouts for the first time.But the iconic Lumumba Vea, meaning “Lumumba lives”, and with his steely gaze, raised right arm and motionless stance throughout a football match continued to live on as DR Congo’s unofficial 12th man with another fan taking his place at the Mercedez-Benz Stadium.That’s the extent of popularity of football’s newest football superfan!With his uncanny resemblance to the country’s first democratically elected Prime Minister, Patrice Emery Lumumba, Mboladinga has been playing Lumumba Vea at Congo’s matches since 2013.While playing a symbol of the fans’ unwavering support to the Congolese national football team, it’s a tribute to the man who who civil rights activist Malcolm X lauded as “the greatest Black man who ever walked the African continent” in 1964.He was a symbol of Congolese nationalism during its struggle against Belgian colonial rule, which began when King Leopold II established the Congo Free State in 1885, and continues to be remembered over 65 years after his death despite being in power for just three months. Lumumba was killed by a firing squad after a military coup in 1961 at the age of 35.Lumumba Vea, however, shot into fame at the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations ‌finals in Morocco when the football world took note of his statue-like demeanour atop a pedestal during every DR Congo match. So much so that the national team players petitioned the Congolese President Felix Tshisekedi to include him in the team’s official contingent.After missing the Congolese World Cup team’s first match against Portugal in Texas, Lumumba was present in the stands for their game against Colombia in Mexico’s Guadalajara, even if the camera cut away when he raised one hand to his mouth and the other placed like a gun to his head, in protest against the Western silence on the current civil was raging Lumumba’s land.Congolese Ambassador in Washington, Kapinga Yvette Ngandu, told ‘Reuters’ she hoped that “he brings his own brand of support to the team” in the knockouts. But whether he will be present when Congo take on the mighty England in their last-32 match in Atlanta is still draped in speculation.But what can be said for sure is that Lumumba Vea, as the name conveys, will live on through some other fan, just like the “Muchachos” song, the “Icelandic Thunderclap” and this edition’s very own “Viking Row” have been living on through the World Cup and the beautiful game.
Article Summary: Michel Kuka Mboladinga has been playing Lumumba Vea at Congo’s matches since 2013. The name means “Lumumba lives”, and with his steely gaze, raised right arm and motionless stance throughout a football match.

Article Title: Manika Batra's Asian Games snub opens bigger debate on TTFI's selection criteria
Article Text:
Manika Batra (Photo X)

Letter Vs Spirit

TOI

Discretion Or Not

Unanswered Questions

When an Asian and Commonwealth Games medallist, three-time Olympian and the poster girl, inarguably, of Indian table tennis is left out of the squad for a major international event, questions are bound to be asked. And even though Manika Batra has herself declared that she’s giving up the search for answers, her exclusion has opened a debate about the future direction of Indian table tennis itself.The women’s squad for the Sept 19-Oct 4 Aichi-Nagoya Asian Games is to be led by Sreeja Akula. It has Yashaswini Ghorpade, Diya Chitale, Sutirtha Mukherjee and 17-year-old Syndrela Das as its other members. Table Tennis Federation of India’s (TTFI) decision to name Manika and Swastika Ghosh as reserves has cast the spotlight on its selection criteria, leading to talk about whether Indian TT is entering a phase where potential is being prioritised over experience.TTFI has maintained the selection process strictly followed its published policy, which assigns 40% weight to international rankings, 50% to domestic performances, and 10% to the discretion of the selection committee, with players ranked inside the WTT top-50 qualifying automatically. At the June 2 rankings cut-off, Manika was world No. 51 and just three WTT points short of making the top-50. While participation in just a single WTT Star Contender event would have earned her five points, not appearing in any tournaments on the domestic circuit is also seen as having hit her chances.In a strongly worded statement, Manika questioned both the process and TTFI’s interpretation of its own rules. “If I do not receive satisfactory answers regarding the basis of this decision, I will be left with no option but to explore all remedies available to me, including legal recourse,” she had said. When TTFI responded, insisting that the team had been picked according to the set criteria, Manika backed away from pursuing the matter legally even as she raised fresh questions over the validity of the selection committee itself. However, she finally signalled the end of her fight on social media, saying, “I have put down my sword. I am mentally exhausted from this fight.”A former India player believes the current policy requires improvement, particularly the importance attached to domestic performances. “International ranking points should carry more weight because they reflect performances against stronger opposition,” the former international toldHowever, the player was equally clear that athletes cannot expect exemptions from laid-down rules. “As athletes, you are not above the rules. You have to safeguard your place in the team. You can’t take selection for granted.”Syndrela’s inclusion is viewed as noteworthy as it wasn’t through discretion but on the basis of merit under the published policy. Her rise reflects the growing depth in Indian women’s TT and the emergence of a younger generation. Whether the selectors also viewed this Asiad cycle as an opportunity to invest in that next generation is a question only the selection committee can answer.Few dispute Manika’s credentials. She remains the only Indian woman to win a Commonwealth Games (CWG) singles gold and has defeated top Chinese players at the biggest meets. But winning a medal at the Asian Games is a big ask. India has never won a singles medal at the continental games, a reminder of the extraordinary standards in Asia, where China, Japan, South Korea and Chinese Taipei dominate.The biggest question surrounding the controversy is over the discretionary clause, which allows room for taking into account exceptional circumstances. Ahead of the 2023 Asian Games, veteran Sharath Kamal was granted a discretionary entry after injury affected his ranking. One can argue that Manika, too, could have been extended such consideration. Her recent journey has been far from smooth. Following the death of her father last year, she took nearly two months away from competition while also managing a recurring back injury. The break affected her world ranking, forcing her to chase points on the demanding WTT circuit.According to sources familiar with the selection committee’s deliberations, the history of legal battles shaped the thinking this time. The selectors believed that overlooking a player who had met the criteria in favour of Manika could open the door to legal challenges from those left out. After all, Diya Chitale had famously moved Delhi High Court in June 2022 to challenge her exclusion from the CWG squad. Chitale was included in the squad following her writ petition, with the selectors dropping Archana Kamath. But Kamath then moved Karnataka HC. The court dismissed her petition, stating that selection criteria fell within the domain of expert committees and that the existing criteria were valid. Similar petitions by Manush Shah and Swastika Ghosh were dismissed by Delhi HC.From the selectors’ perspective, then, the safest course was to apply the policy uniformly, irrespective of stature or past achievements. But if the discretionary provision exists precisely to deal with exceptional circumstances, then when should it be exercised? That’s one of the questions Manika’s exclusion has raised.“I don’t know why the committee didn’t make an exception,” the former India player said. “The team would definitely benefit from having Manika because of her experience.”TTFI can legitimately argue that it followed policy. Equally, critics can question whether a policy designed to eliminate subjectivity has become so rigid that it leaves little room for common sense. The federation has also not publicly explained whether long-term planning determined its thinking.Members of TTFI who were asked for comment said that the official statement from the federation was satisfactory. But Manika’s omission has forced Indian TT to confront some uncomfortable questions. Did the selection policy identify India’s strongest possible team? Was the discretionary provision applied consistently? And, as one generation of stars begins to make way for the next, what should selectors value more: proven pedigree or future potential?
Article Summary: Manika Batra is the only Indian woman to win a Commonwealth Games (CWG) singles gold. TTFI has maintained the selection process strictly followed its published policy, which assigns 40% weight to international rankings, 50% to domestic performances, and 10% to the discretion of the selection committee.

Article Title: UP wins first-ever men's team title at National Athletics Championships, 12 athletes qualify for Asian Games
Article Text:
U.P. Athletic Association ( logo )

Lucknow:

TOI

and Sachin Yadav,

Uttar Pradesh created history by winning the men’s team trophy for the first time at the recently concluded 65th National Inter-State Senior Athletics Championships in Bhubaneswar. Marking the state's strong performance, 12 of the 22 UP athletes who participated in the championships have been selected for the Asian Games to be held in Japan in Oct.Uttar Pradesh’s men’s team finished with 71 points as the state contingent returned with 20 medals, including nine gold, eight silver and three bronze.Talking to, Uttar Pradesh Athletics Association secretary Narendra Kumar said the achievement marked a landmark moment for athletics in the state. “This is the first time in Uttar Pradesh's athletics history that the state has won the men's team trophy at the National Inter-State Senior Athletics Championships. All credit goes to our athletes, who worked extremely hard,” Kumar said.“We ensured that athletes received timely information about events. We also organised two-day meets so that athletes could get proper rest between events, in line with AFI rules.This helped them deliver strong performances,” he added.Kumar said the UPAA conducted 11 state-level competitions along with two national meets during 2024-25 and five state meets till June 2026, helping athletes gain valuable competition exposure.He also credited the Uttar Pradesh govt's sports quota policy for providing job security to athletes. “The state govt's provision of government jobs through the sports quota has had a positive impact. The Athletics Federation of India also sends elite athletes abroad for training exposure, while some private foundations are supporting top athletes,” Kumar said.Kumar said Uttar Pradesh has a rich athletics tradition, with several prominent athletes, including Gulab Chand and Bahadur Prasad, emerging from the eastern region of the state. However, he noted that western Uttar Pradesh currently dominates track and field events.“At the same time, athletes such as javelin throwers Rohit Yadavand long jumper Shahnawaz Khan, have emerged from eastern Uttar Pradesh,” he said.Asian Games-bound athletes Prachi Chaudhary and Rohit Yadav said the support of the Uttar Pradesh govt had played a major role in boosting athletes' confidence.“The Uttar Pradesh govt has played a crucial role by giving us job security. When our future is secure, our morale is high, and that helps us produce strong results,”Prachi said.Javelin thrower Rohit Yadav said, “The govt is offering gazetted jobs and substantial cash rewards, which motivate us like fuel. Our sports infrastructure has also improved significantly.”
Article Summary: Uttar Pradesh created history by winning the men’s team trophy for the first time at the recently concluded 65th National Inter-State Senior Athletics Championships. 12 of the 22 UP athletes who participated in the championships have been selected for the Asian Games to be held in Japan in Oct.

Article Title: Watch: Indian cricketer Yashasvi Jaiswal flaunts 'Messi' jersey during France vs Sweden FIFA World Cup match
Article Text:
Yashasvi Jaiswal at the New York New Jersey Stadium

Poll Vote & Share your view Should Yashasvi Jaiswal have been included in the ODI squad? Yes, he deserves a chance. No, Rohit Sharma is more experienced. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Amid the rising football fever during the ongoing FIFA season, India cricketer Yashasvi Jaiswal was spotted enjoying the France vs Sweden Round of 32 match at the New York New Jersey Stadium in East Rutherford.The Instagram reel was captioned "Bucket List", highlighting the cricketer's long-standing desire to watch a FIFA fixture and his interest in football.Even though France and Sweden were playing, Jaiswal showed where his loyalties lay by wearing Lionel Messi 's Argentina No. 10 jersey to the match, reflecting his admiration for the football icon while soaking up the competition's atmosphere.Watch:Away from the Indian squad, Jaiswal appeared to be making the most of his time off.The opener was left out of the squad following the inclusion of veteran batter Rohit Sharma, giving rise to much speculation, as Rohit has been under scrutiny for a long period.A day after scoring an unbeaten 110 against Afghanistan in Chennai, the 24-year-old found himself dropped from India's ODI squad for the series against England.Since then, Jaiswal has been away from competitive action.Being one of only six batters to score a century in all three formats for the Men in Blue, Jaiswal enjoyed another successful phase with the bat.Jaiswal is expected to return to action in India's upcoming two-match Test series against New Zealand in November.
Article Summary: Yashasvi Jaiswal spotted enjoying the France vs Sweden match at the New York New Jersey Stadium. The 24-year-old was dropped from India's ODI squad for the series against England. The opener is expected to return to action in India's upcoming two-match Test series against New Zealand in November.

Article Title: MLC 2026: Washington Freedom beat LA Knight Riders, jump to fourth in standings
Article Text:
Mark Chapman (Photo by @WSHFreedom on X)

Poll Vote & Share your view What was the key factor in Washington Freedom's victory? Bowling performance Fielding efforts Batting partnership 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

Washington Freedom produced a dominant all-round performance to defeat Los Angeles Knight Riders by six wickets and climb from the bottom of the Major League Cricket 2026 points table to fourth place.Their bowlers and fielders set up the victory by restricting LAKR to just 108 in 17.5 overs on a sluggish surface.After opting to bat, the Knight Riders struggled to build momentum. Colin Munro scored 25, while Matthew Tromp made 21 and Rovman Powell contributed a fighting 30.However, Washington's disciplined bowling attack kept picking up wickets at regular intervals. Saurabh Netravalkar and Mitchell Owen starred with three wickets each, while Ben Dwarshuis and Ian Holland chipped in with two wickets apiece.Washington's sharp fielding and excellent catching also played a key role in dismantling the LAKR batting line-up.Chasing a modest target of 109, Washington suffered two early setbacks as Sunil Narine dismissed Mitchell Owen and Andries Gous, briefly giving the Knight Riders hope of a comeback.However, captain Steven Smith remained calm under pressure and found the perfect partner in Mark Chapman.The pair stitched together a crucial 62-run partnership, taking the game away from LAKR. Chapman played an aggressive knock of 34 off 30 balls before falling to Andre Russell , but by then Washington were firmly in control.Smith anchored the chase brilliantly, remaining unbeaten on 40 off 44 deliveries and guiding his side home with almost three overs to spare.The comprehensive victory not only boosted Washington Freedom's net run rate but also lifted them into the top four of the standings.Despite Narine's impressive spell of 2 for 25, the Knight Riders were unable to defend their below-par total as Washington completed a comfortable win.
Article Summary: Washington Freedom defeated Los Angeles Knight Riders by six wickets. The win sees them climb from the bottom of the Major League Cricket 2026 points table to fourth place. Washington's bowlers and fielders set up the victory by restricting LAKR to just 108.

Article Title: UPT20 League to return to Lucknow amid over Green Park infra, technical concerns?
Article Text:
UPT20 League to return to Lucknow

Lucknow:

The Uttar Pradesh Cricket Association (UPCA) is likely to shift the upcoming Uttar Pradesh T20 League from Green Park Stadium in Kanpur to the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Stadium in Lucknow, barely weeks after announcing Kanpur as the host venue.The UPCA had, during a press conference in Kanpur on May 13, declared that the fourth edition of the league would be held at Green Park.However, the decision was reviewed following a technical assessment of the venue ahead of the tournament, scheduled to be held in Aug.UPCA sources claimed that the stadium’s ageing infrastructure, inadequate floodlighting, limited spectator capacity and concerns over rain-related disruptions have prompted the change of venue.“Since the conclusion of the India-Afghanistan international match, UPCA officials have been carrying out a comprehensive technical assessment of the playing conditions at Green Park Stadium in view of the proposed three-week-long tournament,” a source said.The assessment found that the existing floodlighting system falls short of the standards required for televised matches. “The broadcasting team requires a minimum illumination of 1,700 lux for high-quality telecast, whereas the existing floodlights at Green Park generate only around 1,400 lux,” he said.The association also cited a recent report by the public works department restricting the stadium’s spectator capacity to around 14,000, significantly lower than its earlier capacity, creating operational and commercial constraints for the organisers.Another major concern was the stadium’s vulnerability to waterlogging during the monsoon. Green Park has been struggling with inadequate drainage, resulting in delays in restoring playing conditions after heavy rain.With the tournament scheduled during the peak monsoon season and forecasts indicating prolonged rainfall in Aug, officials fear repeated interruptions over the three-week tournament.UPCA sources said the combination of technical, infrastructural and weather-related challenges left the organisers with little option but to relocate the tournament to Ekana Stadium, which offers superior drainage, modern floodlighting and significantly higher seating capacity.
Article Summary: The Uttar Pradesh Cricket Association (UPCA) is likely to shift the upcoming Uttar Pradesh T20 League from Green Park Stadium in Kanpur to the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Stadium in Lucknow. The decision was reviewed following a technical assessment of the venue ahead of the tournament, scheduled to be held in Aug.

Article Title: Shah Rukh Khan unveils LA Knight Riders stadium as cricket gears up for LA Olympics 2028
Article Text:
Shah Rukh Khan (PTI Photo)

A landmark venue for cricket in America

Eyes on Los Angeles 2028 Olympics

NEW DELHI: Shah Rukh Khan celebrated a major milestone for cricket in the United States with the official unveiling of the LA Knight Riders Cricket Ground in Pomona, California.The new venue represents a significant step in the sport's expansion in North America and is expected to play a crucial role in the build-up to cricket's return to the Olympics at the 2028 Summer Olympics. The stadium will serve as the home ground of the Los Angeles Knight Riders franchise and is set to host several key matches in the coming years.Expressing his excitement, Shah Rukh Khan shared an emotional message on social media, describing the opening of the stadium as the fulfilment of a long-held dream.The Bollywood superstar, who co-owns the Knight Riders franchise, highlighted the broader vision behind the project and thanked those who supported the initiative.He wrote, "What started as a dream… turns into reality today. Welcoming you all to the Knight Riders Cricket Ground, LA. A place built not just for sports but also entertainment and for families… and memories that last forever. Special thanks to Jaybhai who has been too kind through this journey @JayShah, & @ICC and @Sanjog_G for being so supportive. This is for LA, for cricket fans across the globe, and for the Knight Riders family.Believe in Purple and Gold. Welcome to the home of @LA_KnightRiders."The newly opened stadium will host its first competitive cricket match during the ongoing Major League Cricket season.It will become the permanent home of the Los Angeles Knight Riders, giving local cricket fans an opportunity to experience top-level franchise cricket in Southern California for the first time.The venue features modern facilities and has been designed to cater not only to cricket matches but also to entertainment events and family experiences.A total of seven matches are scheduled to be played at the ground during the upcoming Major League Cricket season, marking the beginning of a new chapter for the sport in the region.With cricket steadily gaining popularity in the United States, the launch of a dedicated international-standard venue is being viewed as a major boost for the game's future.The importance of the LA Knight Riders Cricket Ground extends beyond franchise cricket. The venue is expected to play a key role when cricket returns to the Olympic stage at the 2028 Los Angeles Games.As a result, the stadium is likely to become one of the sport's most important destinations globally over the next few years, serving both professional cricket and the wider growth of the game in the United States.
Article Summary: Shah Rukh Khan unveils LA Knight Riders Cricket Ground in Pomona, California. The ground will serve as the home ground of the Los Angeles Knight Riders franchise. The venue is expected to play a key role when cricket returns to the Olympic stage at the 2028 Los Angeles Games.

Article Title: Watch: 'Kya kar raha hai yaar?' - Abhishek Sharma left frustrated after mix-up with Ishan Kishan
Article Text:
Abhishek Sharma left frustrated after mix-up with Ishan Kishan

Poll Vote & Share your view Who impressed you the most in India's innings despite the early troubles? Abhishek Sharma Shreyas Iyer Shivam Dube 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

India suffered an early setback in the first T20I against England after Ishan Kishan was run out following a major mix-up with opening partner Abhishek Sharma.The dismissal came just two balls into Kishan's innings, adding to his misery after he was also run out in India's previous T20I against Ireland.Chasing a quick single, Kishan set off after nudging the ball towards midwicket, but Abhishek, after seeing Harry Brook intercept the ball, decided against the run.The decision was referred to the third umpire, and while the replay was being checked, Abhishek's frustration was clearly visible.During their brief exchange, Abhishek was heard asking Kishan, "Tu pahuncha ki nahin pahuncha, kya kar raha hai yaar Ishan? (Did you make it or not? What are you doing, yaar, Ishan?)"Watch:Kishan's desperate dive back to the crease was not enough as England completed the run-out, leaving India struggling after losing both Sanju Samson and Kishan inside the first two overs.Despite the early confusion, Abhishek Sharma bounced back brilliantly, smashing 59 runs off just 24 balls, including six fours and four sixes.He shared an important 82-run partnership with captain Shreyas Iyer , who anchored the innings with a 68 off 47 deliveries.Abhishek also became the fastest player in T20I history to complete 100 sixes in terms of balls faced.A late unbeaten 42 off 21 balls from Shivam Dube helped India post a competitive total of 189/7 in 20 overs, while England pacer Saqib Mahmood impressed with figures of 3/33.However, persistent rain, which began towards the end of India's innings, prevented England from starting their chase.With conditions failing to improve, the match was eventually abandoned, and the teams will now head to Manchester for the second T20I on July 4.
Article Summary: India lost Ishan Kishan and Sanju Samson in the first two overs. Abhishek Sharma was also run out in India's previous T20I against Ireland. Sharma hit 59 runs off just 24 balls, including six fours and four sixes.

Article Title: Jos Buttler scripts history for England, joins Quinton de Kock in elite T20I list
Article Text:
Chester-le-Street [England], Jul 01 (ANI): India's Harshit Rana gets stumped by England's wicketkeeper Jos Buttler during the 1st T20I, at Riverside Ground in Chester-le-Street on Wednesday. (ANI Photo)

Match Abandoned

Poll Vote & Share your view Which aspect of T20 cricket do you enjoy the most? High-scoring matches Fast-paced wickets Player milestones 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

India vs England, 1st T20I - Playing XIs



England:

India:

Despite the rain washing out the opening T20I against India, England wicketkeeper Jos Buttler achieved a significant personal milestone by completing 100 dismissals in T20 Internationals.Buttler reached the landmark when he produced a lightning-fast stumping to dismiss Harshit Rana off the bowling of Adil Rashid during India's innings. The former England skipper was quick to gather Rashid's googly after Rana charged down the pitch looking for a big hit. Beaten by the turn, the Indian batter failed to get back in time as Buttler whipped off the bails in a flash, registering his 100th T20I dismissal behind the stumps.With the feat, Buttler became only the second wicketkeeper in T20I history and first for England to complete 100 dismissals, joining South Africa's Quinton de Kock, who leads the list with 117 dismissals.India's first T20I against England at Chester-le-Street ended in a no-result after persistent rain prevented England from beginning their chase of 190. After electing to bat, India recovered from 6/2 courtesy of a blistering 59 off 24 balls from Abhishek Sharma and a composed 68 off 47 from captain Shreyas Iyer, who also crossed the milestone of 5,000 international runs.Shivam Dube's unbeaten 42 off 21 provided the finishing flourish as India posted 189/7, while Saqib Mahmood starred with the ball for England, claiming 3/33. However, heavy rain after the innings break forced the match to be abandoned without a ball being bowled in England's innings.Phil Salt, Jos Buttler (wk), Harry Brook (c), Jacob Bethell, Tom Banton, Sam Curran, Will Jacks, Liam Dawson, Saqib Mahmood, Adil Rashid, Luke Wood.Sanju Samson, Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan (wk), Shreyas Iyer (c), Tilak Varma, Shivam Dube, Axar Patel, Harshit Rana, Ravi Bishnoi, Arshdeep Singh, Varun Chakaravarthy.
Article Summary: Buttler becomes only the second wicketkeeper in T20I history and first for England to complete 100 dismissals. India recovered from 6/2 courtesy of a blistering 59 off 24 balls from Abhishek Sharma and a composed 68 off 47 from captain Shreyas Iyer.

Article Title: Rain washes out India-England T20I opener after Shreyas Iyer, Abhishek Sharma power visitors to 189/7
Article Text:
ENG vs IND 1st T20I Abandoned Due to Rain (Image: BCCI)

Partnership for Abhishek and Shreyas

Rain played spoilsports

Brief Scores:

India's opening T20I against England at the Riverside Ground in Chester-le-Street was abandoned without a ball being bowled in the second innings after persistent rain left the outfield unfit for play.After winning the toss, India opted to bat first and recovered from a disastrous start to post a competitive 189/7 in their allotted 20 overs, thanks to an explosive half-century from Abhishek Sharma and a captain's knock from Shreyas Iyer. However, the weather had the final say as continuous rain prevented England from beginning their chase of 190.India's innings began on a shaky note as Saqib Mahmood struck early to remove Sanju Samson, while Ishan Kishan was run out following a mix-up, leaving the visitors struggling at 6/2.With India under pressure, Abhishek Sharma launched a breathtaking counterattack. The left-hander took England's seamers apart with a fearless display of strokeplay, racing to his fifty in just 20 deliveries. Along the way, he also became the fastest batter from a Full Member nation to complete 100 sixes in T20 Internationals.Abhishek and Iyer added 82 runs for the third wicket to wrest back control of the innings.The opener eventually fell for a scintillating 59 off 24 balls, an innings studded with six fours and four sixes, after Sam Curran trapped him lbw.Following Abhishek's dismissal, skipper Shreyas Iyer assumed responsibility in the middle overs. Mixing calculated aggression with sensible strike rotation, the right-hander compiled a composed 68 off 47 deliveries, including six boundaries and a six. During his innings, Iyer also crossed the landmark of 5,000 international runs, becoming the latest Indian batter to achieve the feat.Although Saqib Mahmood returned to dismiss Iyer and finish with impressive figures of 3/33, India found valuable late runs through Shivam Dube. The all-rounder remained unbeaten on 42 off just 21 balls, striking three sixes and two fours to propel the visitors to 189/7.England never had the opportunity to begin their reply. Heavy rain arrived shortly after the innings break and continued throughout the evening, with the ground staff unable to prepare the playing surface before the cut-off time. As a result, the match was officially abandoned without a single delivery being bowled in England's innings, forcing both teams to settle for a no-result.India 189/7 in 20 overs (Shreyas Iyer 68, Abhishek Sharma 59, Shivam Dube 42*; Saqib Mahmood 3/33).
Article Summary: India's opening T20I against England at the Riverside Ground in Chester-le- Street was abandoned without a ball being bowled. After winning the toss, India opted to bat first and recovered from a disastrous start to post a competitive 189/7 in their allotted 20 overs. However, the weather had the final say as continuous rain prevented England from beginning their chase of 190.

Article Title: India opt to bat in 1st T20I vs England; Varun Chakaravarthy returns, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi waits for debut
Article Text:
Varun Chakaravarthy (Getty Images)

Shreyas Iyer (India captain):

Harry Brook (England captain):

Playing XIs



England:

India:

India captain Shreyas Iyer won the toss and elected to bat first in the opening T20I of the five-match series against England at the Riverside Ground, Chester-le-Street. The visitors have made one notable change from the squad that toured Ireland, with Varun Chakaravarthy returning after recovering from a foot injury. Teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, meanwhile, will have to wait for his international debut as India opted for an experienced playing XI featuring three specialist spinners.Speaking after the toss, Iyer said the team had moved on from the disappointing Ireland series and was eager to make a fresh start."We'll bat first. It's a great opportunity for us. My second series as a captain, pretty much excited. Got what we wanted and had a practice session yesterday to acclimatize. The Ireland series is history. There's a decent amount of grass on the wicket, so it's difficult to predict how it'll play, but our intent will remain high. We're going with three spinners."England captain Harry Brook admitted he would have preferred to bowl first but expressed confidence in his side's balanced lineup."We would've bowled first, but we're happy with the way things have gone.It looks like a good wicket. The mood in the camp is really good and we're excited to get the series underway. We've got plenty of experienced players like Jos Buttler, Phil Salt and Sam Curran, who I can always lean on."England have rested Jofra Archer and Josh Tongue after their recent involvement in the Trent Bridge Test, paving the way for Luke Wood and Saqib Mahmood to feature in the series opener.Phil Salt, Jos Buttler (wk), Harry Brook (c), Jacob Bethell, Tom Banton, Sam Curran, Will Jacks, Liam Dawson, Saqib Mahmood, Adil Rashid, Luke Wood.Sanju Samson, Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan (wk), Shreyas Iyer (c), Tilak Varma, Shivam Dube, Axar Patel, Harshit Rana, Ravi Bishnoi, Arshdeep Singh, Varun Chakaravarthy.
Article Summary: India captain Shreyas Iyer won the toss and elected to bat first in the opening T20I of the five-match series against England. Teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will have to wait for his international debut as India opt for an experienced playing XI featuring three specialist spinners. England captain Harry Brook admitted he would have preferred to bowl first but expressed confidence in his side's balanced lineup.

Article Title: Bangladesh appoint Towhid Hridoy captain for Zimbabwe T20Is; Litton Das granted LPL leave
Article Text:
Towhid Hridoy named captain, Litton Das misses out (Image: X)

Bangladesh T20I Squad



Bangladesh Tour of Zimbabwe 2026



Bangladesh Tour of Zimbabwe 2026 Schedule

Match Date Venue Local Time 1st ODI July 6 Harare Sports Club, Harare 9:15 AM 2nd ODI July 9 Harare Sports Club, Harare 9:15 AM 3rd ODI July 11 Harare Sports Club, Harare 9:15 AM 1st T20I July 15 Queens Sports Club, Bulawayo 1:30 PM 2nd T20I July 17 Queens Sports Club, Bulawayo 1:30 PM 3rd T20I July 19 Queens Sports Club, Bulawayo 1:30 PM

Bangladesh have named Towhid Hridoy as captain for the upcoming three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe after regular skipper Litton Kumar Das was granted leave to participate in the Lanka Premier League (LPL). The Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) announced a 15-member squad on Wednesday, with all-rounder Mosaddek Hossain earning a recall following his impressive recent form.Litton, along with fast bowler Taskin Ahmed, will miss the T20I series to honour their LPL commitments. The three matches are scheduled to be played at Queens Sports Club in Bulawayo on July 15, 17 and 19.Mosaddek returns to the T20I setup after producing a series of strong performances in the recent ODI series against Australia, where he amassed 157 runs in three matches, strengthening his case for a comeback in the shortest format.The selectors have also recalled Yasir Ali, who is set for his first T20I appearance in nearly three years after last representing Bangladesh during the Asian Games in October 2023. Pace-bowling all-rounder Mohammad Saifuddin also returns after missing the home white-ball series against Australia, while Shamim Hossain has been rested following a string of low scores.Towhid Hridoy (captain), Parvez Hossain Emon, Tanzid Hasan, Mohammed Saif Hassan, Mosaddek Hossain Saikat, Yasir Ali Chowdhury, Nurul Hasan Sohan, Mahedi Hasan, Nasum Ahmed, Rishad Hossain, Shoriful Islam, Mustafizur Rahman , Nahid Rana, Mohammad Saifuddin and Abdul Gaffar Saqlain.Bangladesh's white-ball tour of Zimbabwe begins on July 6, following an eventful one-off Test in Harare. The visitors will first contest a three-match ODI series at the Harare Sports Club before travelling to Bulawayo for a three-match T20I series, with the tour concluding on July 19.
Article Summary: Bangladesh have named Towhid Hridoy as captain for the upcoming three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe. Regular skipper Litton Kumar Das was granted leave to participate in the Lanka Premier League (LPL) The Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) announced a 15-member squad on Wednesday. All-rounder Mosaddek Hossain earns a recall following his impressive recent form.

Article Title: 'I was not utilised properly': Shardul Thakur's explosive claim on Shubman Gill-led England tour
Article Text:
India's captain Shubman Gill with Shardul Thakur (PTI Photo)

Mumbai: India all-rounder and current Mumbai captain Shardul Thakur has alleged that he was "underutilized" in the five-Test series in England last year.Playing under new Test captain Shubman Gill and guided by head coach Gautam Gambhir , India drew the five-Test series 2-2.Thakur, who has taken 33 Test wickets in 13 Tests at 31.03, played in just two Tests in the series, in which he took two wickets at 72.00, at an economy rate of 5.33. Across the two Tests, Thakur bowled 27 overs while conceding 144 runs.Thakur bowled just six overs in the first innings of the first Test at Leeds, in which he conceded 38 runs without taking a wicket, as India bowled 100.4 overs. In the second innings, he took two for 51 in 10 overs, as India bowled 82 overs. India lost that Test by five wickets, as England made 465 and 373 for five.Recalled for the fourth Test at Manchester, Thakur conceded 0-55 in 11 overs as England piled up 669 in the first innings. The match ended in a draw.Interestingly, on all three occasions, Thakur bowled the fewest overs among all the regular Indian bowlers.“After the 2024-25 season, the kind of season I had (he took 35 wickets in nine matches at 22.62), I think I could have played a few more games and literally made a difference.When I played in England, I was not utilised properly in bowling. In batting, yes, it was my mistake that I played a loose shot in Leeds (he was out for 1 and 4). But in Manchester, I batted beautifully (he scored 41 in the first innings). I would say it was overcast, the ball was swinging, and there was one length where it was bouncing oddly. So I survived that spell. And India still put up a very strong total (358) in the first innings. And then the pitch became flat and then we were also able to save the game. But had I not had that innings when the second new ball arrived, then probably the result could have been different also,” Thakur told a select group of reporters on the sidelines of the Mumbai Cricket Association’s ‘2026-27 season launch’ at the MCA’s BKC facility on Wednesday.Ruing the fact that he was “underbowled” in the England series due to some “calculation error”, the 34-year-old Shardul said, “I would say I was being underbowled and it was about being bowled in the wrong phases. So there was some calculation error that I would say.”Did the ‘Palghar Express’ have a word with the captain to clarify the situation with regard to him? “No, I mean, usually you don't have a word with the captain or the coach because all the focus is on the game. Whenever you are asked to do something, all you think about is, okay, now is my chance and I want to deliver for the team. And while doing that, sometimes it happens, sometimes it doesn't happen,” Thakur replied.Ruing the fact that he was taken off the attack even after taking the wickets of Ben Duckett and Harry Brook in the 55th over of the Leeds Test, as England chased down 373 successfully while losing just five wickets, Thakur said, “So, if you see that in Leeds, I got those two back-to-back wickets and India was back in the game, but then I was stopped again. So that's where I could have bowled a longer spell, maybe got another wicket and then the other bowlers would have come in and we could have won that game also. So again, ifs and buts. ”“My comments are not going to change the scenario of that game and the result of that game. But yeah, I mean, you don't end up having these kinds of conversations usually with the captain and all. You basically take whatever has been asked of you to do,” Thakur said.During one of his press conferences in England during the Test series last year, Thakur had hinted at being unhappy about being bowled far less than he wanted to.However, despite his bitter experience while playing for the country the last time around, Thakur is hopeful that he will get a chance to play for India again in the future. “So, it's not that I have not made any contributions when I played for India last time. But yes, I think I deserve a few more opportunities. But it's okay. Like I said, hope is still alive. And in the near future, if they ever ask me to play for India again, I would be ready,” he said.
Article Summary: Shardul Thakur claims he was 'underutilized' in the five-Test series in England last year. The 34-year-old bowled 27 overs while conceding 144 runs across the two Tests. ThakUR bowled just six overs in the first innings of the first Test at Leeds.

Article Title: Big boost for England! Nat Sciver-Brunt returns for the T20 World Cup semi-final against South Africa
Article Text:
Nat Sciver-Brunt returns ahead of semi-final against South Africa (Image: X)

England vs South Africa in Oval

England have received a major boost ahead of their Women's T20 World Cup semi-final against South Africa, with captain Nat Sciver-Brunt declared fit to return after recovering from injury. The experienced all-rounder was forced to retire hurt during England's group-stage victory over Ireland in Southampton after aggravating an injury she had initially sustained while playing domestic cricket in late April.The setback had already ruled her out of England's key preparation series against India and New Zealand before the World Cup. Despite the injury concerns, Sciver-Brunt featured in both warm-up matches, scoring 57 against India after managing just three runs against Australia. She has also played a crucial role in England's unbeaten World Cup campaign, contributing an unbeaten 46 against Sri Lanka before retiring on 48 against Ireland.Speaking at the pre-match press conference at Kennington Oval, Sciver-Brunt confirmed she is ready to lead England in Thursday's knockout clash."We literally threw everything at it. I'm really happy with where I am physically. Myself and the medical team are really happy with where we're at.Physically I'm able to take part in all of the game, so from that point of view I'm really happy," Nat added.England Women will play South Africa Women at The Oval on Thursday, July 2, 2026, at 10:30 PM IST (5:00 PM local time). This high-stakes matchup is the second semi-final of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026, with the winner booking a spot in Sunday's final at Lord's against Australia. England enters the knockout stage unbeaten, having comfortably steamrolled through all five of their Group B fixtures. The Proteas qualified second in Group A after a clutch four-wicket win against Bangladesh, combined with an ideal result from Australia defeating India.
Article Summary: England captain Nat Sciver-Brunt is fit to face South Africa in the semi-final. The experienced all-rounder was forced to retire hurt during England's group- stage victory over Ireland in Southampton. The setback had already ruled her out of England's key preparation series against India and New Zealand.

Article Title: After Ben Stokes' retirement, Harry Brook says he'd 'be happy to take' England Test captaincy
Article Text:
Harry Brook and Ben Stokes (Image: X)

The 27-year-old admitted that leading England in the longest format would be the greatest honour of his career.

India tour of England

Match Date Venue Time (IST) 1st T20I July 1, 2026 Riverside Ground, Chester-le-Street 10:00 PM 2nd T20I July 4, 2026 Old Trafford Cricket Ground, Manchester 7:00 PM 3rd T20I July 7, 2026 Trent Bridge, Nottingham 10:00 PM 4th T20I July 9, 2026 County Ground, Bristol 10:00 PM 5th T20I July 11, 2026 The Rose Bowl, Southampton 7:00 PM 1st ODI July 14, 2026 Edgbaston, Birmingham 5:30 PM 2nd ODI July 16, 2026 Sophia Gardens, Cardiff 5:30 PM 3rd ODI July 19, 2026 Lord's Cricket Ground, London 3:30 PM

England are set to begin a new chapter in Test cricket following the retirement of long-time captain Ben Stokes, who called time on his international career during Day 4 of the third Test against New Zealand. With the leadership role now vacant, England's white-ball captain Harry Brook has emerged as the frontrunner to succeed Stokes.While England's immediate focus shifts to the white-ball series against India, comprising five T20Is and three ODIs, beginning with the first T20I today, Brook addressed the possibility of becoming England's next Test captain during the pre-match press conference."Look, it would be a great honour to do it, it'd be a privilege to do it, to captain England in the highest format of our game, The pinnacle, I think it is. Playing Test cricket is the greatest thing that I've ever done in my life and it's a dream and something that I've always wanted to do since I could speak. Look, it's not up to me, that decision, but if I got offered it then I'd be happy to take it," Brook said.Despite being widely regarded as Ben Stokes' deputy, Brook supported the ECB's decision to hand the captaincy to Joe Root for the second Test against New Zealand following Stokes' retirement, insisting it was the right call."I think the decision that was made was the right one. Making Rooty captain last week was definitely the right decision. He has always been there for the ECB. He has been a stalwart for English cricket. He's, in my opinion, the best batter to ever play Test cricket. The ECB needed him that week as well and he stepped up,'' he added."My job that week was to just try and help him as much as possible. Lots of conversations: obviously we're at first and second slip, so we're always chatting about how we can have an effect on the game. But I definitely feel ECB made the right decision," Brook stated.
Article Summary: Harry Brook is the frontrunner to succeed Ben Stokes as England's Test captain. The 27-year-old admitted that leading England in the longest format would be the greatest honour of his career. Brook supported the ECB's decision to hand the captaincy to Joe Root for the second Test against New Zealand.

Article Title: Watch: Malik Tillman's stunning free-kick after Folarin Balogun's red card sends USA into FIFA World Cup Round of 16
Article Text:
United States' Malik Tillman (17) celebrates after scoring on a free kick. (AP Photo)

Tillman's stunning strike seals historic win



Watch the goal here:

Pulisic hails fighting spirit after red-card drama



The United States booked their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 in dramatic fashion as Malik Tillman produced a sensational free-kick to seal a 2-0 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina after Folarin Balogun's controversial red card had threatened to derail the hosts' campaign.With the Americans reduced to 10 men and Bosnia pushing for an equaliser, Tillman's moment of brilliance settled the contest and sparked wild celebrations at Levi's Stadium.The USA had looked in complete control after Balogun scored his third goal of the tournament just before halftime. However, the momentum shifted dramatically in the 64th minute when the striker was shown a straight red card following a VAR review after catching Tarik Muharemovic on the ankle.Forced to play the final half-hour with 10 men, the hosts came under increasing pressure before Tillman delivered the decisive blow in the 82nd minute.Standing over a free-kick just outside the penalty area, the midfielder curled a superb effort over the wall. Bosnia goalkeeper Nikola Vasilj managed to get a hand to the ball but could only help it into the net as the stadium erupted.Remarkably, Tillman had changed his right boot shortly before taking the set-piece after struggling with a bloody toe."You never know when it's going to happen. Today, it happened," Tillman said after scoring the USA's first direct free-kick goal at a World Cup since Eric Wynalda against Switzerland in 1994.Captain Christian Pulisic praised the team's resilience after surviving nearly 30 minutes a man down."We had to dig deep for that one. It didn't go exactly to plan with the red card, but that just shows what a good team we are. We said in the hydration break, you know, this is what it takes to be a really strong team. And, we were able to do it," Pulisic said after the match.Head coach Mauricio Pochettino, meanwhile, disagreed with the dismissal of Balogun, and said: "For me? Never a red card."Balogun will now miss the Round of 16 clash against Belgium through suspension, but Tillman believes the squad has enough depth to cope."Of course he's a great player. We have great players who can replace him, and give the best they can, and hopefully score some nice goals as well," Tillman said.The victory gave the United States only their second-ever men's World Cup knockout win and snapped a 10-game winless run against European opposition at the tournament. They will now face Belgium in Seattle on July 6, with a quarterfinal place at stake.
Article Summary: United States beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 to reach World Cup 2026 Round of 16. Malik Tillman scores stunning free-kick in 82nd minute to seal victory. Folarin Balogun sent off for USA in 64th minute after VAR review. Captain Christian Pulisic hails team's resilience after being reduced to 10 men.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup: Will Folarin Balogun miss USA's Round of 16 clash vs Belgium after red card? Rules explained
Article Text:
Referee Raphael Claus shows the red card to United States' Folarin Balogun, unseen, during the World Cup Round of 32 match. (AP Photo)

Balogun sent off after VAR review



Will Balogun play against Belgium?



The United States booked their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 with a hard-fought 2-0 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina, but the celebrations were tempered by a major setback as star striker Folarin Balogun was sent off during the second half.The red card means Mauricio Pochettino's side is now set to be without one of its most influential attackers for the knockout clash against Belgium on July 6 — unless a successful appeal overturns the suspension.Balogun had given the United States the lead just before halftime with his third goal of the tournament before his night took a dramatic turn in the 64th minute.The striker challenged Bosnia defender Tarik Muharemovic for the ball and accidentally stepped on his opponent's ankle. Referee Raphael Claus initially allowed play to continue before reviewing the incident on the pitchside monitor. After the VAR check, Claus upgraded the decision to a straight red card.The dismissal forced the Americans to play the remainder of the match with 10 men, though Malik Tillman later sealed a famous 2-0 victory with a stunning free-kick.The goal was Balogun's third of the tournament, drawing him level with Landon Donovan's tally from the 2010 World Cup and leaving him just one behind Bert Patenaude's record of four goals by an American at a single men's World Cup, set in 1930.Under FIFA's disciplinary regulations, a player shown a straight red card is automatically suspended for his team's next match. As things stand, that means Balogun will miss the United States' Round of 16 meeting with Belgium.However, there remains a slim possibility that the striker could still feature.Following the match, the US Soccer Federation can submit an appeal to FIFA's disciplinary committee seeking to overturn the suspension. If FIFA determines that the dismissal was incorrect or warrants reconsideration, the automatic one-match ban could be rescinded.Should the appeal fail, Balogun will watch from the sidelines as the United States chase only their third-ever World Cup knockout victory.The striker had been one of the standout performers of the tournament before his dismissal. Besides scoring, he had another effort ruled out for offside and repeatedly troubled the Bosnian defence with his movement.Balogun also paid tribute to NBA legend LeBron James by celebrating with the iconic "Silencer" gesture after finding the net. James later acknowledged the celebration on social media.The red card was only the fifth received by a US player at a men's World Cup, adding another dramatic chapter to an eventful night that ultimately ended with the Americans reaching the Round of 16 despite playing more than 25 minutes a man down.
Article Summary: United States beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 to reach World Cup 2026 Round of 16. Folarin Balogun was sent off in the 64th minute after stepping on an opponent's ankle. Referee Raphael Claus initially allowed play to continue before reviewing the incident on the pitchside monitor. After the VAR check, Claus upgraded the decision to a straight red card. The dismissal forced the Americans to play the remainder of the match with 10 men, though Malik Tillman later sealed a famous 2- 0 victory.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Las Vegas Raiders and Kansas City Chiefs linked to $3M Carolina Panthers WR
Article Text:
NFL Trade Rumors: Las Vegas Raiders and Kansas City Chiefs linked to $3M Panthers WR (Getty)

Which teams are interested in Xavier Legette?

Category



Details



Player Name



Xavier Legette



Current Team



Carolina Panthers



Rumored Teams



Las Vegas Raiders, Kansas City Chiefs



Contract Status



Under contract through 2028



2026 Cap Hit



$3.37 million



Trade Likelihood



Moderate



Latest Insider Update



Panthers could listen to offers if Legette slips further down the depth chart



Potential Return



Mid-to-late Day 3 draft pick







What are insiders saying about the NFL trade rumours?

Contract details and salary cap implications

How a potential trade could impact both teams



Xavier Legette entered the NFL carrying first-round expectations. Two seasons later, he's entering one of the biggest years of his career.The Carolina Panthers have reshaped their receiving corps over the past two offseasons, and the competition has only become tougher. Tetairoa McMillan quickly established himself as Bryce Young's go-to target, while Jalen Coker continues to earn a bigger role. The addition of rookie Chris Brazzell II has added another obstacle for Legette, whose inconsistent production has fueled questions about where he fits in Carolina's long-term plans. If Legette struggles to carve out a consistent role during training camp or the opening weeks of the season, rival teams could start making calls. His age, athletic profile and affordable rookie contract would make him an intriguing reclamation project.The Las Vegas Raiders stand out as one of the most logical landing spots. The franchise is beginning a new chapter under head coach Klint Kubiak and rookie quarterback Fernando Mendoza. While the organization has invested heavily in building its offense, the receiver room still lacks an established playmaker with Legette's size and physical traits.A fresh start could benefit both sides, giving Legette an opportunity to rebuild his confidence while providing Las Vegas with another young target to develop alongside its new quarterback.The Kansas City Chiefs present a different kind of fit. Kansas City's passing attack remains one of the league's most dangerous, but questions continue to surround the depth chart behind Patrick Mahomes. Rashee Rice has struggled to stay on the field because of injuries and off-field issues, while Xavier Worthy is still searching for consistency after an uneven start to his career. Legette would not be expected to become the focal point of the offense immediately, but Andy Reid has a long history of maximizing athletic receivers with untapped potential.Panthers Wire's Anthony Rizzuti believes 2026 could determine whether Xavier Legette remains part of Carolina's future. Rizzuti notes that the former first-round pick has struggled with drops and consistency through his first two NFL seasons, while the emergence of McMillan and Coker has pushed him further down the pecking order. With nine career games of 10 receiving yards or fewer, Legette has yet to produce the type of impact Carolina envisioned when it selected him in the first round.Roundtable Sports' Dylan Feltovich echoed a similar view, writing that Legette remains a potential trade candidate if he fails to take a step forward this summer. Feltovich also pointed to the arrival of third-round receiver Chris Brazzell II as another sign that Carolina's front office is continuing to reshape the position group rather than waiting for Legette to develop.Xavier Legette remains on his rookie contract, which runs through the 2028 season. He carries a cap hit of just over $3.37 million in 2026 and is scheduled to earn roughly $1.9 million in base salary. His deal is fully guaranteed and also includes a fifth-year option, giving any acquiring team multiple years of contractual control. That team-friendly contract could increase his value despite an inconsistent start to his career.If Carolina moves on from Legette, it would signal complete confidence in the younger receivers who have climbed the depth chart. The Panthers would also create more opportunities for players like Brazzell and Coker while recovering draft capital from a former first-round investment.For the Las Vegas Raiders, Legette would represent a low-risk addition with room to grow in a developing offense. Kansas City, meanwhile, could view him as another versatile weapon capable of thriving alongside Patrick Mahomes in a system known for reviving talented players who simply need a change of scenery.Nothing suggests a deal is close. Even so, Legette's name is likely to remain part of NFL trade conversations until he firmly secures his place in Carolina's plans.
Article Summary: Xavier Legette entered the NFL carrying first-round expectations. Two seasons later, he's entering one of the biggest years of his career. The Carolina Panthers have reshaped their receiving corps over the past two offseasons. Tetairoa McMillan quickly established himself as Bryce Young's go-to target. Jalen Coker continues to earn a bigger role.

Article Title: "Too many people afraid of being exposed": Jordon Hudson escalates Dianna Russini controversy with cryptic move
Article Text:
Jordon Hudson, Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini (Getty Images)

Why is Jordon Hudson continuing to poke on the Dianna Russini-Mike Vrabel controversy?

Jordon Hudson has once again inserted herself into the ongoing controversy surrounding former NFL insider Dianna Russini and Tennessee Titans head coach Mike Vrabel, this time by amplifying criticism of how the story has been handled inside the sports media. While the investigation into Russini continues to draw attention, Hudson's latest social media activity suggests her focus extends beyond the allegations themselves. Instead, she appears increasingly interested in what she sees as inconsistent media coverage and the reluctance of some journalists to speak publicly about the situation.Hudson's latest reaction came after NFL podcaster Tony Farmer questioned why so many people were reportedly willing to discuss the Dianna Russini-Mike Vrabel situation privately but refused to attach their names to it publicly."Why are so many people willing to talk off the record about the Russini – Vrabel story, but so few are willing to talk on the record? What exactly is the fear?" Farmer wrote on X.NFL writer Crissy Froyd responded with a detailed explanation that quickly gained traction."Too many people doing similar things who are afraid of being exposed, or they’re being threatened with their jobs if they talk," Froyd wrote. "I’ve held freelance roles where I was specifically told not to talk about Russini or that I could be let go.I did anyway, and no, that comment is not referencing USA TODAY."Jordon Hudson reposted Froyd's message, adding another layer to her repeated criticism of how the controversy has been covered. Farmer soon highlighted her activity, writing, "Bill Belichick’s girlfriend Jordon Hudson is stirring the pot – she just retweeted this 👀."The repost fits a pattern that has developed over the past several weeks. Hudson has repeatedly questioned why the story has received limited attention from major media outlets despite continuing developments.One of the biggest moments in the controversy came when police body camera footage contradicted Russini's public account of a traffic stop. Russini had claimed she FaceTimed an NFL head coach to help get out of receiving a ticket. She referenced Minnesota Vikings coach Kevin O'Connell and former New York Giants coach Brian Daboll, saying O'Connell told the officer to let her go because she was a "good citizen."The released footage told a different story. Although Dianna Russini showed text messages involving O'Connell, no FaceTime call was made during the stop, directly contradicting her earlier version of events.Hudson has repeatedly pointed to that episode as an example of what she believes is selective reporting. In an earlier social media post, she openly questioned the lack of coverage, writing, "We’re not talking about this? Why not, @ProFootballTalk?"Her interest in the issue also carries another layer because of her relationship with Bill Belichick. The longtime Patriots coach spent years working alongside Vrabel in New England, where the pair won three Super Bowls together during Vrabel's playing career and six championships overall during Belichick's tenure.Meanwhile, Froyd's own role in the story has also drawn attention. After speaking publicly about the controversy following Russini's resignation from The Athletic, Froyd's contractor agreement with USA TODAY ended. She has continued discussing the issue despite that outcome.Dianna Russini remains under investigation by The Athletic even after leaving the publication, with the findings expected to be released in the near future. Whether larger media organizations choose to give those results significant attention remains an open question. If Hudson's recent activity is any indication, she is likely to keep watching closely and continue weighing in as the story develops.
Article Summary: Jordon Hudson has continued to weigh in on the Dianna Russini-Mike Vrabel controversy. Hudson has repeatedly questioned why the story has received limited attention. She has also criticized the reluctance of some journalists to speak publicly about the situation.

Article Title: Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes' Kansas City Current unveil massive $1.4 billion stadium expansion plans
Article Text:
The Kansas City Current have unveiled a $1.4 billion expansion of CPKC Stadium, increasing capacity from 11,500 to 18,000 seats while adding a parking garage and riverfront development. Minority owners Patrick and Brittany Mahomes continue backing the club's growth. Founded in 2020, the Current have become an NWSL powerhouse, finishing runners-up in 2022, winning the NWSL Shield, and becoming the first team to sell out every home match in 2024.

Kansas City Current reveal ambitious CPKC Stadium expansion

Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes continue backing Kansas City Current's growth

The Kansas City Current are preparing for another major leap after unveiling plans for a $1.4 billion expansion of CPKC Stadium, a project that reflects the club's rapid rise in the National Women's Soccer League. The proposal, presented to the Kansas City, Missouri City Council on June 30, would significantly increase the stadium's capacity while adding new riverfront infrastructure. Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes , who are minority owners of the club, continue to be part of one of women's football's fastest-growing success stories.Just two years after opening the first purpose-built stadium for an NWSL club, Kansas City Current are already planning to nearly double its size. If approved, CPKC Stadium will expand from approximately 11,500 seats to 18,000, allowing thousands more supporters to attend home matches.The proposal goes beyond increasing seating. It also includes a dedicated transient parking garage and a mixed-use riverfront development designed to improve access for fans while supporting continued growth around the venue.The club outlined its vision in an official statement while presenting the proposal to city officials."Today, KC Current presented expansion plans for CPKC Stadium to the KCMO City Council.“As demand for KC Current matches, events and riverfront development continues to grow, outpacing even our initial high expectations, so does the need to expand and improve infrastructure around the stadium — including increased stadium capacity to 18,000; a dedicated transient parking garage; and core riverfront infrastructure that will better serve fans, residents, families and the broader KC community.“What stands today as CPKC Stadium on the riverfront is more than a stadium.“It is proof of what KC can accomplish when a community comes together around bold ideas and a shared vision for the future.“We cannot wait to build the next chapter together.”The project highlights how the club is planning for long-term demand rather than reacting only to current attendance figures.The Current have enjoyed remarkable progress since launching as an NWSL expansion club in December 2020. Originally owned by Angie Long, Chris Long and Brittany Mahomes, the organisation later welcomed Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes as a minority owner, further strengthening its high-profile ownership group.Kansas City was built on an accelerated timeline after the Utah Royals FC stopped operations, and quickly established itself as one of the strongest teams in the league. Last season, the club was the NWSL runner-up and won the NWSL Shield, further cementing its place as a title contender.Support from the fans has been comparable to the team’s success on the pitch. The Current sold out all regular season home games for the first time in franchise history in 2024, underscoring the rise in popularity of women’s football in Kansas City.That sustained demand is a major reason behind the latest expansion proposal. Rather than waiting for capacity to become a long-term issue, the club is investing in a larger stadium experience while enhancing surrounding infrastructure for future supporters and community development.With Patrick and Brittany Mahomes continuing to invest in the club's vision, the proposed expansion represents another milestone for a franchise that has quickly become one of the NWSL's model organisations, both on and off the field.
Article Summary: Kansas City Current reveal ambitious CPKC Stadium expansion plans. The proposal would increase capacity from 11,500 to 18,000 seats. It also includes a dedicated transient parking garage and a mixed-use riverfront development. Minority owners Patrick and Brittany Mahomes continue backing the club's growth.

Article Title: Did Reece Weaver's husband force her to retire from the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders? The truth behind the rumors
Article Text:
Reece Weaver has denied claims that her husband, Will Allman, pressured her to retire from the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders. She said the decision was entirely her own after months of reflection and that Allman supported her regardless of her choice. Weaver explained she felt fulfilled after three seasons and is excited to begin a new chapter. She also confirmed she is not leaving dance behind and plans to continue exploring new opportunities.

Reece Weaver shuts down rumors about husband Will Allman

Why Reece Weaver decided to leave the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders

Reece Weaver is clarifying the rumors on the web that her husband, Will Allman, was the deciding factor in her retirement from the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders. The season three finale of America’s Sweethearts had fans of the show flooding social media with theories about the departure of one of the most popular members of the squad. Weaver has now directly responded to the claims, insisting the decision was hers alone.After Weaver announced she was leaving the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders, many fans speculated that Allman had encouraged her to step away. The theories gained traction because the couple's marriage has been featured prominently on America's Sweethearts, along with their Christian faith and Allman's involvement in managing Weaver's growing influencer career.One moment from Season 3 showed Allman reflecting on how business responsibilities had begun affecting their relationship."These are really cool opportunities that we can't really pass up, but it got to a point where I was telling Reece what to do,” he said. “'You need to do this. This is the deadline for this.' It became more like a business partnership rather than a marriage."Those scenes led some viewers to conclude that Weaver's retirement had not been entirely her choice.She firmly rejected that idea.“It makes me frustrated, because that could not be more far from the truth,” she said.Weaver explained that Allman never pressured her and instead stood behind whichever path she chose.“He was so supportive, no matter what decision I made.” She added: “No matter what decision I had, he was just going to support either way. I always go to him for options and outcomes. He could kind of process what we could do if we went this way, and what we could do if we went the other direction.”Allman also dismissed the criticism, saying:“Reece is a bunch of people's favorites. Those people are going to find something else to blame if I wasn't there, so it doesn't really affect me a whole lot.”According to Weaver, thoughts of retirement began long before the season ended. Entering her third year with the squad, she already felt she had achieved everything she hoped to accomplish.“Going into season 3, my third year on the team, I just had so much gratitude. My cup was filled to the brim even going into this year.”She continued:“My cup was getting really, really full. I went into my last game not thinking and knowing it was my last, but I kind of treated it as it was my last, so I could really just soak it all in. I think I really needed that sense of closure for my mental state.”Weaver admitted telling longtime director Kelli Finglass and choreographer Judy Trammell about her decision was one of the hardest conversations she has ever had. Still, she said the choice ultimately brought her peace after months of reflection.Looking ahead, Weaver made it clear that retirement from the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders does not mean retirement from dance."I still want to continue to dance and just explore what life has to offer.”Instead of focusing on public speculation, Weaver is embracing a new chapter, confident that following her own instincts was the right decision, with her husband supporting her every step of the way.
Article Summary: Reece Weaver has denied claims that her husband, Will Allman, pressured her to retire from the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders. She said the decision was entirely her own after months of reflection. Weaver explained she felt fulfilled after three seasons and is excited to begin a new chapter.

Article Title: D.J. Moore bought out an entire store’s stock of his shirts in a huge gesture for Bills fans
Article Text:
(Image via Getty: DJ Moore)

D.J. Moore’s generous gesture quickly became a viral moment among Bills fans

Bills Mafia appears to be embracing D.J. Moore before the season even begins

D.J. Moore is already making a strong first impression in Buffalo

Winning over a new fanbase usually takes time. Some players do it with touchdowns. Others do it with big plays in primetime. D.J. Moore may have just done it before even playing a meaningful snap.The new Buffalo Bills wide receiver is making headlines after a heartwarming gesture that instantly caught the attention of Bills Mafia. Moore reportedly bought out an entire store’s stock of his shirts worth $1,200, only to turn around and give them away to fans.And naturally, Bills fans loved every second of it.The story spread quickly online after fans learned Moore had purchased every available shirt featuring his name from the team store. Instead of keeping them or using them for promotions, Moore reportedly handed them out to fans.That simple gesture immediately created buzz.In today’s NFL, fans love seeing players build genuine connections beyond the field. Small moments like this can say a lot about a player’s mindset, personality, and how seriously they take the relationship with their supporters.For Moore, this was more than just a giveaway.It felt like a clear message to Buffalo fans: he is excited to be here and ready to embrace the culture.And Bills Mafia is not just any fanbase. Buffalo’s loyal supporters are known across the NFL for their passion, energy, and dedication. They ride hard for players who fully buy into the city and team culture.That is why Moore’s move resonated so strongly.Fan reactions online were overwhelmingly positive. Many praised Moore for making such a thoughtful move, with some calling it one of the coolest offseason gestures from any Bills player this year.Others pointed out that winning over Bills Mafia is not easy, but moments like this go a long way.The excitement around Moore has already been growing as the Bills prepare for another major season. With expectations once again high in Buffalo, fans are eager to see how Moore fits into the offense and what kind of impact he can make.His arrival adds another exciting weapon to the Bills’ offense and gives fans plenty of reasons to feel optimistic heading into the season.At the end of the day, football will decide how successful Moore’s Buffalo chapter becomes. Big catches, touchdowns, and clutch performances will matter most.But first impressions matter too.And right now, Moore seems to understand exactly how to connect with Bills fans.By turning a simple store visit into a memorable moment for supporters, he gave fans something to talk about long before kickoff.If this is how Moore is starting his Buffalo journey, Bills Mafia has every reason to be excited.

Article Text:
